job_statement: When managing access to Argo CD, I want to configure role-based access control policies, so I can ensure users have appropriate permissions for their responsibilities.
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to grant appropriate access
Reduce likelihood of unauthorized access
Ensure compliance with security policies
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 122-185: Configuring Argo CD RBAC section
notes: Default instance has no access except kube:admin. Custom instances have read-only by default.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when I need to grant specific permissions to users, I want to edit the RBAC policy in the Argo CD CR, so I can assign appropriate roles without granting cluster-admin access.
desired_outcomes: Complete RBAC configuration in under 5 minutes
Avoid granting excessive permissions
Ensure user access aligns with organizational roles
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 142-185: Configuring user level access procedure
notes: Red Hat SSO cannot read group information, so RBAC must be configured at user level

section: Configuring SSO for Argo CD using Dex
job_statement: When managing multiple users, I want to configure single sign-on for Argo CD, so I can centralize authentication and reduce password management overhead.
desired_outcomes: Minimize authentication complexity for users
Reduce time spent managing credentials
Ensure consistent identity across OpenShift and Argo CD
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 188-409: Configuring SSO for Argo CD using Dex
notes: Dex is installed by default. Can use OpenShift OAuth or external OIDC providers.

section: Configuration to enable the Dex OpenShift OAuth Connector
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when I want to leverage OpenShift users and groups for Argo CD authentication, I want to enable Dex with OpenShift OAuth, so I can avoid maintaining separate user directories.
parent_job: Configure single sign-on for Argo CD
desired_outcomes: Complete SSO configuration in under 10 minutes
Ensure users can authenticate with existing OpenShift credentials
Avoid maintaining duplicate user databases
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 329-365: Configuration to enable the Dex OpenShift OAuth Connector procedure
notes: Cannot use spec.sso and spec.oidcConfig simultaneously

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when users have ClusterRoleBinding roles, I want to map them to Argo CD roles through OpenShift groups, so I can grant admin access to authorized users.
parent_job: Configure single sign-on for Argo CD
desired_outcomes: Ensure cluster-admin users have admin access to Argo CD
Minimize manual role assignment overhead
Maintain role consistency across OpenShift and Argo CD
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 373-400: Mapping users to specific roles procedure
notes: Workaround for users with direct ClusterRoleBinding - create groups and add users

section: Configuring SSO for Argo CD using external OIDC providers
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when I need to integrate with an external identity provider, I want to configure direct OIDC integration for Argo CD, so I can authenticate users without using Dex as an intermediary.
parent_job: Configure single sign-on for Argo CD
prerequisites: Obtain client ID and secret from provider
desired_outcomes: Successfully integrate with enterprise identity providers
Minimize authentication latency by avoiding intermediaries
Ensure proper scope and claim mapping
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 533-665: Configuring SSO for Argo CD using external OIDC providers
notes: Provider-agnostic, works with any OIDC-compliant provider. Cannot use with spec.sso simultaneously.

section: Verifying OIDC login for Argo CD
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, after configuring OIDC integration, I want to verify that authentication is working correctly, so I can ensure users can access Argo CD with their identity provider credentials.
parent_job: Configure single sign-on for Argo CD
desired_outcomes: Confirm authentication flow works end-to-end
Verify role-based permissions are applied correctly
Identify and resolve authentication issues quickly
notes: Tests the complete authentication flow including provider redirect

section: Managing local users in Argo CD
job_statement: When I need API tokens for automation or have small teams without SSO, I want to manage local user accounts in Argo CD, so I can provide programmatic access without configuring external identity providers.
desired_outcomes: Minimize complexity for automation scenarios
Reduce overhead of maintaining external identity providers
Ensure API tokens are securely managed and automatically renewed
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 698-1262: Managing local users in Argo CD
notes: Operator manages user accounts, JWT tokens, lifetimes, renewal, and cleanup

section: Creating local users in Argo CD
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when I need to provide API access for service accounts or automation scripts, I want to create local users in Argo CD, so I can generate API tokens without configuring SSO.
parent_job: Manage local user accounts in Argo CD
desired_outcomes: Complete local user creation in under 5 minutes
Ensure tokens are generated and stored securely
Enable automation without SSO complexity
notes: Operator reconciles changes and creates secrets/tokens automatically

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when managing API tokens for local users, I want to configure automatic renewal and rotation policies, so I can balance security with operational continuity.
parent_job: Manage local user accounts in Argo CD
desired_outcomes: Ensure automation continues without token expiration interruptions
Minimize manual token rotation overhead
Maintain security through time-limited tokens
notes: Supports automatic renewal and manual rotation. Disable apiKey to cleanup secrets.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when user access needs change, I want to disable or remove local users without losing their configuration, so I can temporarily revoke access or permanently clean up accounts.
parent_job: Manage local user accounts in Argo CD
desired_outcomes: Revoke access immediately when needed
Preserve configuration for temporary disablement
Clean up all resources when users are permanently removed
notes: Disabled users retain tokens but cannot authenticate. Removed users trigger complete cleanup.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator or automation engineer, when I need to access Argo CD APIs, I want to retrieve local user tokens from Kubernetes secrets, so I can authenticate service accounts and automation scripts.
parent_job: Manage local user accounts in Argo CD
prerequisites: Have local users with API keys created
desired_outcomes: Retrieve tokens quickly from Kubernetes secrets
Ensure tokens are base64-decoded correctly
Successfully authenticate automation scripts and service accounts
evidence: accesscontrol_usermanagement-combined.adoc -> Lines 1060-1109: Token storage and access section
notes: Tokens stored in {username}-local-user secrets. Values are base64-encoded.
